chunks.text: IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, BENCH AT SUKKUR Crl. Bail Application No.S-195 of 2024 (Muhammad Yousif Malik v. The State) Mr. Shafique Ahmed Laghari, Advocate for applicant. Mr. Shafi Muhammad Mahar, Deputy P.G for the State. Date of Hearing & Order: 29-04-2024 O R D E R MUHAMMAD IQBAL KALHORO, J.: Allegation against the applicant is that he along with other co-accused, named in FIR, committed theft of iron pipes, iron angles, electricity lines of 11000 volts on 13.01.2024 and was arrested by ASI Muhammad Hassan Bhayo of P.S, Jhangro- Sukkur on spy information along with a Datsun he was travelling in with such articles. His accomplices, however, are said to have escaped. Hence, FIR. 2. His case for bail, as argued by his counsel, is that he has been falsely implicated by the police and no one from SEPCO, articles of which are alleged to have been stolen by the applicant, has come forward to make complaint or be a witness in this case to verify such fact. These grounds have been conceded by learned Deputy P.G for the State. 3. Accordingly, this bail application is allowed on the grounds that no one from SEPCO, whose articles allegedly applicant has stolen, has come forward and to make a complaint or be a witness in this case to verify that these articles belong to them and were available at the given place from where had been stolen. Prima facie, the case of further enquiry is made out. Consequently, applicant is admitted to post-arrest bail subject to his furnishing solvent surety in the sum of Rs.50,000/- Crl. Bail Application No.S-195 of 2024 Page 2 of 2 (Fifty Thousand) with P.R bond in the like amount to the satisfaction of the trial Court.

Consequently, applicant is admitted to post-arrest bail subject to his furnishing solvent surety in the sum of Rs.50,000/- Crl. Bail Application No.S-195 of 2024 Page 2 of 2 (Fifty Thousand) with P.R bond in the like amount to the satisfaction of the trial Court. The observations made hereinabove are tentative in nature and shall not influence the trial Court while deciding the case on merits. JUDGE Ahmad
